criteria: Implement per-tensor quantization for weight tensors with global min/max calibration
Implement per-channel quantization computing independent scales per output dimension
Support configurable channel dimension (dim=0 for Linear and Conv2d output channels)
Use broadcasting for efficient per-channel quantize/dequantize operations
Achieve at least 30% lower MSE with per-channel vs per-tensor on heterogeneous weights
Compute and report per-channel statistics (scale range, MSE per channel, variance)
Document memory footprint comparison including scale storage overhead
Verify compression ratio approximately 4x for INT8 vs FP32 including scale overhead
Support both symmetric (zero_point=0) and asymmetric per-channel quantization
Validate that per-channel scales sum to correct storage overhead (8 * num_channels bytes)
Implement QuantizationConfig dataclass with bit_width (2/4/8/16), symmetric (bool), signed (bool), computed q_min/q_min fields, and validation rejecting invalid bit_widths and per_channel=True

Implement compute_scale_zero_point(r_min, r_max, q_min, q_max, symmetric, eps) returning (scale, zero_point) with symmetric forcing zero_point=0 and asymmetric computing zero_point=round(q_min - r_min/scale) clamped to valid range
Implement quantize(tensor, scale, zero_point, q_min, q_max) applying affine transform q=clamp(round(r/scale + zero_point), q_min, q_max) preserving input shape
Implement dequantize(quantized, scale, zero_point) applying inverse transform r=scale*(q - zero_point) returning approximation on quantization grid
Implement get_qrange(bit_width, signed) returning correct integer ranges for all valid configurations (INT4: -8 to 7, INT8: -128 to 127, etc.)
Implement TensorQuantizer class with calibrate(tensor), quantize(tensor), dequantize(quantized), quantize_dequantize(tensor) methods and calibrated state tracking
TensorQuantizer.quantize raises RuntimeError if called before calibrate
Implement compute_quantization_error(original, reconstructed, metric) supporting MSE, MAE, and max error metrics
Implement analyze_quantization_quality(tensor, config) returning scale, zero_point, mse, mae, max_error, original_range, unique_quantized_values
Quantization MSE below 0.01 for torch.randn(10000)*0.5 with INT8 symmetric quantization
Symmetric quantization always produces zero_point exactly equal to 0
Values exactly on quantization grid reconstruct within half-scale tolerance
All quantized values clamped to [q_min, q_max] preventing integer overflow
Support tensors up to 10M elements with quantize+dequantize in under 20ms
